name: Pygeum
summary: Pygeum has moderate evidence for modest improvement in BPH urinary symptoms and urinary flow.
description: Pygeum has moderate evidence for modest improvement in BPH urinary symptoms and urinary flow.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v9.2: Older meta-analysis suggests possible BPH benefit, but a pygeum/nettle combination RCT showed placebo-like results.
claimMap.notes: v9.2: Older meta-analysis suggests possible BPH benefit, but a pygeum/nettle combination RCT showed placebo-like results.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Pygeum dose is product-specific; older studies often used extract preparations for ≥30 days

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

contraindications: other benign-prostatic-hyperplasia medications such as alpha-blockers or 5-alpha-reductase inhibitors (theoretical additive effect warrants monitoring)
sensitive stomach (can cause nausea or abdominal pain or constipation)
